verdicts.description: Mount the front bracket: align to the front chassis interface, square to the vehicle centerline with bores aligned.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Front bracket aligned square to the vehicle centerline with bores aligned to the front chassis interface.
verdicts.reasoning: Worker aligns and holds the front bracket square to the centerline with bores aligned at 00:00:35.

verdicts.description: Install the front bracket screw by hand and tighten to firm finger torque; HOLD POINT confirms the fastener is the screw, not an acorn nut.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Threaded bracket screw (not an acorn nut) fully engaged and seated flush at firm finger torque, confirmed by head shape.
verdicts.reasoning: Worker installs an acorn nut in place of the threaded bracket screw. The two are visually similar and not interchangeable; substituting the acorn nut for the bracket screw is a documented error mode that has escaped automated inspection. The hold-point head-shape confirmation was not performed.

verdicts.description: Install the rear pulley on the rear axle first, before any rear wheels.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Magenta rear pulley installed and seated on the rear axle prior to installing the rear wheels.
verdicts.reasoning: Worker proceeds to the rear wheel stack without first installing the rear pulley. A missing rear pulley is a documented low-detectability error state once the wheels are installed; the pulley must always be installed before the wheels.

verdicts.description: Install the front wheel assembly, left side: assemble the stack in order wheel, washer, acorn nut; tighten to firm finger torque.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Left front stack assembled wheel-washer-acorn nut with washer present and acorn nut at firm finger torque.
verdicts.reasoning: Left front stack assembled in the correct order with washer present and acorn nut finger-tightened at 00:02:50.

verdicts.description: Install the front wheel assembly, right side: assemble the stack in order wheel, washer, acorn nut; tighten to firm finger torque.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Right front stack assembled wheel-washer-acorn nut with washer present and acorn nut at firm finger torque.
verdicts.reasoning: Worker installs the right front wheel and acorn nut without the washer behind the wheel. Omission of a wheel washer is the documented low-detectability error on this build; the assembly appears acceptable without it.

verdicts.description: Install the rear wheel assembly, left side: wheel, washer, acorn nut; tighten to firm finger torque.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Left rear stack assembled wheel-washer-acorn nut with washer present and acorn nut at firm finger torque.
verdicts.reasoning: Left rear stack assembled correctly with washer present and acorn nut finger-tightened at 00:04:10.

verdicts.description: Install the rear wheel assembly, right side: wheel, washer, acorn nut; tighten to firm finger torque.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Right rear stack assembled wheel-washer-acorn nut with washer present and acorn nut at firm finger torque.
verdicts.reasoning: Right rear stack assembled correctly with washer present and acorn nut finger-tightened at 00:04:45.

verdicts.description: Wheel assemblies verification: a washer behind every wheel (4x), all four acorn nuts at firm finger torque, rear pulley present and seated, wheels rotate freely.
verdicts.compliance_criterion: All four wheels rotate freely with no axial play; rotation check performed with hands clear.
verdicts.reasoning: Worker spins all four wheels with hands clear at 00:05:20; each rotates freely with no obvious axial play.

verdicts.description: Compare the build to the state strip component by component; every tracked component must match the checkpoint (state 13).
verdicts.compliance_criterion: Build matches the 11-component state strip for the checkpoint with no mismatch.
verdicts.reasoning: The build does not match the state strip: the rear pulley is absent (STEP-003) and one front wheel washer is missing (STEP-005). Per the state model any mismatch is a nonconformance requiring work to stop and the rework path in chapter 8.
